zh.Basingstoke, Hampshire, England: 英國哈普爾巴辛斯托可
zh.lifeSummary: 當Lord Richard John Rich於1470年出生在英國米德萊可倫敦時，他的父親Thomas Rich38歲，母親Elizabeth Mayne30歲。他於1485年與Joan Dineley在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1503年9月8日在他的家鄉享年33歲。

zh-hans.Basingstoke, Hampshire, England: 英国哈普爾巴辛斯托可
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lord Richard John Rich于1470年出生在英国米德萊可倫敦时，他的父亲Thomas Rich38岁，母亲Elizabeth Mayne30岁。他于1485年与Joan Dineley在英国英格兰结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1503年9月8日在他的家乡享年33岁。

nameMeanings.name: Rich
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname for a wealthy man (or perhaps in some cases an ironic nickname for a pauper), from Middle English, Old French riche ‘rich, wealthy’, a word of ancient Germanic origin, akin to ancient Germanic rīc ‘power(ful)’.
English: from the Middle English personal name Rich, a pet form of any of the post-Conquest names beginning in Rich-, such as Richer and especially Richard . Compare Rick .

English: either a topographic name from Middle English riche(Old English ric) ‘stream’, signifying one who lived beside a stream, as at Glynde Reach (Sussex), or perhaps a habitational name from the (now lost) village of Riche (Lincolnshire).

variants.name: Ritchie
meanings: Scottish: from a Scottish and northern English pet form of the personal name   Rich  , a short form of   Richard  . This surname is also common in Ulster.

variants.name: Ritch
meanings: English and Scottish (Orkney): either a variant of Rich , or perhaps from a short form of Ritchie , itself a diminutive based on Rich.

Altered form of German Ritsch, probably from a short form of an ancient Germanic personal name based on rīc ‘power(ful)’ or hrōd ‘fame, renown’; or an altered form of Swiss German Rütsch, Ruetsch, from Alemannic short forms of Rudolf .

variants.name: Riches
meanings: English (Norfolk and Suffolk): variant of Rich , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Richer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Normand, Armand, Fernand, Herve, Laurent, Marcel, Aurele, Donat, Elphege, Emile, Flore, Gilles.

English (East Anglia), French, and German: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements rīc ‘power(ful)’ + hari, heri ‘army’. The name was introduced into England by the Normans in the form Rich(i)er, Ricier, but was largely absorbed by the much more common Richard .

Altered form of French Héricher: from the ancient Germanic personal name Harigari, composed of the elements hari ‘army’ and gari (from garwa) ‘ready, eager’ or gari ‘spear’, or a variant of Hérissé, a nickname for someone with spiky hair, from the adjective hérissé, a derivative of the verb se hérisser ‘to stand on end’.

variants.name: Riche
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Pierre, Ghislaine, Philippe, Yanick, Yvon.
French: nickname for a rich man or perhaps an ironical name for a pauper, from Old French riche ‘rich’. Compare Leriche .

variants.name: Richins
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Ricun, a diminutive of Rich , itself a pet form of Richard , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s. It appears that a dialectally different (Norman or Picard) form of the French name, with medial /-k-/, also survived in England for a time alongside forms with original /-tʃ-/.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

en.lifeSummary: When Lord Richard John Rich was born in 1470, in London, Middlesex, England, his father, Thomas Rich, was 38 and his mother, Elizabeth Mayne, was 30. He married Joan Dineley in 1485, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 8 September 1503, in his hometown, at the age of 33.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lord Richard John Rich est né en 1470, à Londres, Middlesex, Angleterre, son père, Thomas Rich, avait 38 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Mayne, avait 30 ans. Il a épousé Joan Dineley en 1485, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 8 septembre 1503, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 33 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lord Richard John Rich 1470 in London, Middlesex, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Thomas Rich 38 und seine Mutter Elizabeth Mayne 30. Er hat 1485 in England, United Kingdom Joan Dineley geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 8. September 1503 im Alter von 33 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lord Richard John Rich è nato il nel 1470 nel London, Middlesex, England, il suo padre, Thomas Rich, aveva 38 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth Mayne, aveva 30 anni. Lui sposato Joan Dineley nel 1485, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 8 settembre 1503, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 33 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Lord Richard John Richが1470年にイギリスミッドレセックスロンドンで生まれた時，父Thomas Richは38歳，母Elizabeth Mayneは30歳であった。1485年にイギリスイギリスでJoan Dineleyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1503年9月8日に故郷で33歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Lord Richard John Rich(이)가 1470년 영국 미들레세스 런던에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas Rich(은)는 38세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Mayne(은)는 30세였다. 그는 1485년 영국 잉글랜드에서 Joan Dineley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1503년 9월 8일 그의 고향에서 33세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lord Richard John Rich nasceu em 1470, em London, Middlesex, Inglaterra, seu pai, Thomas Rich, tinha 38 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Mayne, tinha 30 anos. Ele casou-se com Joan Dineley em 1485, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 8 de setembro de 1503, em sua cidade natal, com 33 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lord Richard John Rich родился в 1470 г. в местности Лондон, миддлесекс, Англия, его отцу по имени Thomas Rich было 38 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Mayne, 30. Он женился на женщине по имени Joan Dineley в 1485 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер 8 сентября 1503 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 33 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lord Richard John Rich nació en 1470, en London, Middlesex, Inglaterra, su papá, Thomas Rich, tenía 38 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Mayne, tenía 30 años. Se casó con Joan Dineley en 1485, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 8 de septiembre de 1503, en su pueblo, a la edad de 33 años.
